name: Curried Shellfish Pot Pie
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup lobster meat, steamed or boiled for 5 minutes to loosen, then removed and chopped
3/4 pound young carrots, scraped, trimmed, cut into 2-inch lengths, blanched for 2 minutes
4 ounces shiitake mushrooms (stems removed and thickly sliced)
1 pound all-butter puff pastry (defrosted in the refrigerator if frozen)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 425°F.
2. Invert a 3-quart oval or rectangular casserole, preferably earthenware or porcelain, onto a piece of parchment or wax paper. Trace the shape, adding a 1-inch border all around, then cut it out.
3. In a large heavy skillet, heat butter over medium high heat until it foams. Whisk in flour, adjust the heat to medium low, and continue cooking until the flour is light-cream colored, about 2 minutes.
4. Stir in clam broth, sherry, cream, and lemon juice. Adjust the heat to medium high, and slowly bring the mixture to a boil, stirring constantly. Cook for 30 seconds longer. It will be quite thick.
5. Remove pan from heat. Stir in ginger, garlic, curry powder, red pepper, salt, and black pepper. Let mixture cook slightly.
6. Fold in parsley, lobster meat, shrimp, carrots, mushrooms, almonds, peas, and onions. Scrape filling into casserole.
7. On a slightly floured work surface, roll puff pastry to a thickness of 1/4 inch. Cut out using the paper template. In the center of the pastry, cut an air hole about 1/2 inch in diameter.
8. Place pastry over casserole, pressing down on the sides to seal. Decorate top with strips or shapes (optional).
9. Brush top with egg wash. To prevent liquid from boiling over onto the crust, make a steam vent by rolling a 3-inch strip of heavy aluminum foil around a wooden spoon handle. Insert gently into hole. Remove spoon.
10. Place casserole on cookie sheet and transfer to middle of oven. Bake for 20 minutes, then reduce heat to 375°F and continue baking for 20-to-25 minutes longer, or until pastry is puffed and golden brown.
11. Remove pan; let rest with vent inside for 15-to-20 minutes to keep crust from becoming soggy.
